reviews.author: Daniela Inzua
reviews.text: The pagoda is very beautiful, and the cave is honestly stunning — super, super impressive and definitely worth visiting. It’s a really special place. That said, I do think there should be better control around cleanliness. Some visitors don’t seem to know where to dispose of their trash, and leaving garbage in such a beautiful place feels disrespectful. Hopefully this improves because the site truly deserves better care. Overall though, it’s absolutely worth the visit.

reviews.author: Zhang Qingye
reviews.text: Visited Bich Dong Pagoda during my solo trip to Ninh Binh and really enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere. The pagoda is built into the hillside, with several levels to explore, and offers lovely views of the surrounding countryside. The combination of natural scenery and historic architecture makes it a unique and calming experience. The walk up is manageable and well maintained, with clear steps and paths, making it easy to explore at your own pace. Even when travelling alone, it felt safe and quiet, allowing time to reflect and take photos. Highly recommended for solo travellers or anyone looking to experience a serene, cultural site in Ninh Binh.

reviews.author: Philip Belfast
reviews.text: It was ok tbh. The best thing about it is when you get to the front gate , walk to the right and take the path around the outer side wall. It leads up and over a hill into a stunning valley. At the top left hand corner is a guy who, for 30k. Show you around an amazing cave system owned by him. One of the best I’ve seen.

reviews.author: Clare Newman
reviews.text: Recommend visiting. It’s a beautiful little temple area to wander around. Entry is free but parking attendants will try to charge you 30k for scooter parking. You can negotiate to 10k or better still, park at the last restaurant before the entry, the guy is lovely and offered us free parking. To show appreciation, we bought some water from him. We later ate some Banh mi here beside the lake and it was good too.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: This ancient pagoda is absolutely amazing! It looks intriguing from the outside with its iconic gate and stone bridge. I was even more surprised once I stepped inside, an entire complex of temples and cave. The way the three levels—Lower, Middle, and Upper pagodas—are built directly into the mountainside is truly unique. Simple, tranquil, and solemn—highly recommended!

reviews.author: Đinh Thị Hoài Thương
reviews.text: Bich Dong Pagoda is a beautiful and sacred temple complex with a long history and calm atmosphere. There are three pagodas built on different levels of the mountain, surrounded by lush greenery, lotus ponds, and cool fresh air — a perfect place to find peace and quiet. You can see a few monks and local people praying, as well as elderly ladies keeping the temple clean and selling small souvenirs nearby. The entrance is free, and parking costs only 20,000 VND. Next to the pagoda, there’s a path leading up to “Hanh Phuc Lake” (Happiness Lake) — try climbing up for a lovely view of the mountains and countryside! A truly authentic and spiritual stop in Tam Coc, perfect for those who love culture, nature, and tranquility.

reviews.author: Juliana Chin
reviews.text: A place worth visiting, an ancient pagoda first built in the 15th century nestled in a serene tropical hill/ mountain. As part of the journey to the 3rd level you move through a cave that has amazing structures. Surrounding the ancient structure were calm waters of water lilies which unfortunately bloom mainly in the morning. We drove to Ninh Binh from Hanoi which is roughly a 1 hour 45 drive. The pagoda is then only a few mins drive from Tam Coc. On a Sunday the crowd was decent. No entry tickets required and as always, be respectfully quiet visiting a sacred place for locals. When we arrived it started drizzling - if you do hit wet weather, so be careful as the stone steps are slippery.

reviews.author: Ewan Kilpatrick
reviews.text: Very beautiful setting nestled in amongst the limestone cliffs. Free to enter, but recommend if arriving by bike or scooter to park at one of the cafes along the roadside before reaching the place itself as we saw several people having issues being haggled for payment after parking bicycles at the end, despite no signage about a charge to park up there. Easy to park anywhere slightly earlier and can grab a wee drink from them to support too. Can climb up the temples up a few steps to get some elevated views too. Worth stopping by

reviews.author: Beth Slipper
reviews.text: Beautiful scenery, the highlight of the visit. It is free to enter, a very small climb to inside the cave and then the top. There are small tourist shops surrounding it and easy to access via grab rides. A good spot to visit when staying in Tam Coc.

reviews.author: Ski Man
reviews.text: Very pleasant visit. Don’t be put off by the reviews about parking scams - just be assertive and cycle past the ladies trying to flag you down. At the end there is a bicycle park and the lady was giving out tickets. She didn’t charge us but others seemed to pay 5000vdn per bike - keep some perspective as that is only 15p so I can’t see how anyone can really complain!! The temple and caves were very pleasant and we visited in the afternoon which was not crowded.

reviews.author: Daniel Sin
reviews.text: Went to Bich Dong Pagoda with a tour guide and was good explaining that history of this place. There are 3 temples to visit, the walk to the temple can be steep and may be slippery during raining season. You will have to walk into a bat cave to get to the second temple. The view from the top temple is awesome well worth the walk up. Not too high just a moderate climb up.

reviews.author: Alex Ross
reviews.text: Such hypocrites! They have a sign explaining laws of karma. In one of the lessons, they talk about people who keep animals in cages. But they have two VERY BIG dogs e VERY SMALL cages. It's absurd. Especially since the temple has so much free space where the dogs could be without disturbing others. In my country, people who treat animals with such cruelty go to jail. It's a crime.

reviews.author: Tô Quang Long
reviews.text: Ancient beauty with a breathtaking view Bich Dong Pagoda is incredibly majestic and deeply historic. You can really feel the ancient atmosphere here. The architecture is unique, but the highlight for me was the water lily pond at the gate—it was blooming beautifully and made for a perfect photo. A very serene and spiritual place.

reviews.author: Martin
reviews.text: Beautiful temple - the best bit is along the path through the 'Parking Area'. There is a short climb up some steps and then it opens out onto a lake, which is absolutely stunning. Very few people head this way - had it all to myself even when the pagoda was busy. Free parking at the cafes / restaurants - DO NOT park in the 'Parking Area' as you will have to pay.

reviews.author: Sarah Grund
reviews.text: It's a super cool place. Did not expect to be walking through a cave to see the temple. Also, loved the little dog, it was acting like our little guide.

reviews.author: Cassandra Tho
reviews.text: Really scenic space, with water bodies around it. It was a good workout climbing the stairs. It can be quite steep for some. At the top, it can be slippery because of algae due wet conditions.

reviews.author: MML Reviews
reviews.text: We love FREE!!! Make sure you have some non slip footwear... I did have flip flops on. We hiked to the top and it didn't even seem to be hard work despite me not being fit! Views and the temples are just beautiful Highly recommend

reviews.author: Joseph Allen
reviews.text: Our favourite pagoda in Nin Binh. Get lost in the winding caves and hike to the top for a breathtaking view. You could easily spend two hours exploring Bich Dong!

reviews.author: Chloé W (Walk_with_clo)
reviews.text: Nice place to visit, entrance free, parking at 50 (negociable). Don't miss the little valley behind, follow the path on the right, climb up and follow the trail

reviews.author: Roizy Sprei
reviews.text: This is a nice filler because it's completely free to get inside. There are lots of vendors right outside that offer to sell you goods & to park your bike for $$. Get food & drink at the Gao restaurant & park there instead. The food there is amazing. The pagoda is cool because you hike up some stairs to get to cave with bats and views.

reviews.author: All in One Vietnam
reviews.text: Great time to travel is for Spring and winter. It is crowded abit in summer cause of local travellers. Free ticket and walking insite upto to of moutain, We chosed moving behind moutain where we found very nice valley too.

reviews.author: Grayson
reviews.text: Outstanding. Pagoda, cave, and lake. Was raining when I visited, but it was still very nice. Bike/scooter parking is a pricey at 10k, other places were 3k-5k.

reviews.author: Sabri Kürüm
reviews.text: A very impressive building which is combined with the mountains behind it. A light climb and you get a very nice view

reviews.author: Alexandra H
reviews.text: BE AWARE OF SCAMS the entrance is totally free! We were forced to park in this area. The man in the picture behaved very nicely and asked us to pay 50,000 per person as a ticket fee, offering free parking. Later we discovered that the entrance was indeed free. Together with another couple we confronted him and got our money back. This situation taught us to learn the rules and entrance fees before visiting any places of interest.

reviews.author: Minh Le
reviews.text: Entry to the temple is free, there’s NO COST. Parking wise, you can park before you pass steel barrier before the brown uniform guy telling you to go this parking spot. I was charged $20k vnd per bike. Which was fair enough. The hike and walking through the pagoda was marvellous and beautiful.

reviews.author: G T
reviews.text: The pagoda's ok, but alone I didn't think that worth a visit. It's the climb up the hill that makes this worthwhile. Great views from the top of the hill, but it's a strenuous climb with fairly uneven stairs.

reviews.author: Jonathan Weaver
reviews.text: Be patient and you can get great pics Temple, I am told is 700+ years old Free to visit

reviews.author: Mav&Char
reviews.text: Parking scam! A man in a green shirt waving a Vietnam flag with a whistle will try to usher you into the parking zone - here it’s 20k to park a bicycle and 50k to park a scooter. Ignore the man and park where I have drawn the arrow in the photo for free, or at any of the cafes nearby.

reviews.author: Yair Meged
reviews.text: Nice temples but not that interesting. If you saw different temples so don’t waste your time

reviews.author: Alberto Jiménez
reviews.text: Beautiful entrance-fee padoga nearby tam coc. Good to do it by yourself .Highly recommend it. We went by foot with a 1 y.o baby. Was a lovely walk around & took us about 40 min approx ( depending on your pace ). However and due to heavy rains the main entrance was bit flooded but OK to pass by.. just mind your step. After it we made a diversion throughout the lake, strongly recommended as well
